metadata.title: Crackoï — Fine Art Prints by Eric Lamblin
metadata.description: Discover fine art prints by Eric Lamblin, underwater photographer in Réunion Island. Whales, dolphins, landscapes — museum-quality prints.

block1.heading: Eric Lamblin, Ocean Photographer
block1.text: For over 20 years, Eric Lamblin has been capturing the wild beauty of the Indian Ocean. Based in Réunion Island, he dives into the heart of the waters to immortalize rare moments: the gaze of a humpback whale, the dance of a pod of dolphins, the light piercing through the surface.

block2.heading: Réunion Island, Land of Contrasts
block2.text: Between volcanoes and coral reefs, Réunion Island offers a unique playground for photography. From the marine depths to the peaks of Piton des Neiges, every image tells a story of preserved nature and extraordinary encounters.

block3.heading: An Authentic Artistic Approach
block3.text: Each print is a work of art in its own right. Eric meticulously selects his images, favoring emotion and connection with the subject. His photographs are not mere snapshots — they are windows opening onto a world that few are lucky enough to see.

block4.heading: In the Intimacy of Whales
block4.text: Every year, humpback whales migrate to the warm waters of Réunion Island to give birth. Eric accompanies them, observes them, photographs them with deep respect. These images bear witness to a unique relationship of trust between man and animal.

mockupPreview.heading: Your Photo at Home
mockupPreview.text: Each print is produced on museum-quality Fine Art paper. Visualize the result directly in your interior with our preview tool.

book.title: In the Intimacy of Whales
book.subtitle: Eric Lamblin's photography book
book.description: An exceptional journey to the heart of the Indian Ocean, meeting the humpback whales of Reunion Island. Through over 200 breathtaking photographs, Eric Lamblin immerses us in the intimacy of these ocean giants.
book.description2: Each page reveals the majesty of these fascinating creatures, captured in their natural habitat with rare proximity and sensitivity. Images that bear witness to 20 years of exploration and passion.
book.featuredTitle: A few pages from the book
book.featuredSubtitle: A glimpse of the photographs that make up this unique work

about.title: Eric Lamblin
about.subtitle: Underwater Photographer — Reunion Island
about.bio1: For over 20 years, Eric Lamblin has explored the waters of the Indian Ocean, camera in hand. Based in Reunion Island, he has become one of the most recognized underwater photographers on the island.
about.bio2: Specializing in humpback whale and marine life photography, Eric captures moments of rare beauty. His images bear witness to infinite patience and deep respect for the underwater world.
about.bio3: His work has been published in numerous magazines and exhibited worldwide. He is also the author of the book "In the Intimacy of Whales".
